methods.description: Procedure for allocating rats into experimental groups and administering treatments to study the effects of HExt and lead acetate.

steps.description: A total of twenty-four male rats were allocated randomly into four experimental groups of six rats each.
steps.searchable_summary: randomly allocate 24 male rats into four groups of six
outputs: four groups of six rats each

steps.description: Group I (Con): control animals given a standard diet and water.
steps.searchable_summary: assign control group to standard diet and water

steps.description: Group II (HExt): animals receiving HExt via gastric gavage daily for 3 weeks at a dosage of 2 mg kg −1 body weight (b.w.).
steps.searchable_summary: administer HExt via gastric gavage daily for 3 weeks

steps.description: Group III (LA): animals receiving lead acetate at a dosage of 5 mg kg −1 b.w. via daily intraperitoneal injection (i.p.) for 3 weeks.
steps.searchable_summary: administer lead acetate via intraperitoneal injection daily for 3 weeks

steps.description: Group IV (LA + HExt): animals receiving lead acetate at a dosage of 5 mg kg −1 b.w. (i.p.) and HExt at a dosage of 2 mg kg −1 b.w. via daily gastric gavage for 3 weeks.
steps.searchable_summary: administer lead acetate and HExt daily for 3 weeks

methods.description: Procedure for collecting and preparing blood and organ samples from rats for biochemical analysis.

steps.description: At the end of the experimental period, all animals were anesthetized and then sacrificed via decapitation to minimize handling stress.
steps.searchable_summary: anesthetize and sacrifice animals via decapitation

steps.description: The trunk blood was collected. The serum was separated via centrifugation (1500× g, 15 min, 4°C), and then frozen and stored at −20°C until use for biochemical assays.
steps.searchable_summary: collect trunk blood, centrifuge to separate serum, freeze and store

steps.description: A portion of each organ was homogenized (10% w/v) using an Ultra Turrax homogenizer in 0.01 M ice-cold sodium potassium phosphate buffer (pH 7.4).
steps.searchable_summary: homogenize organ samples in phosphate buffer

steps.description: Homogenates were centrifuged at 10,000× g for 20 min at 4°C. The resulting supernatants were aliquoted and stored at −80°C for biochemical analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: centrifuge homogenates, aliquot supernatants, store for analysis
